company.name: Microsoft

marketCap.rationale: Computed from Microsoft's $21.00 IPO price and 24,715,113 shares outstanding after the offering in the prospectus.

company.description: Enterprise software and cloud infrastructure giant spanning productivity, developer platforms, and operating systems.

overview.title: The office landlord of the enterprise century
paragraphs: Microsoft still captures a remarkable amount of value from being the default digital office, identity provider, and enterprise coordination fabric. It has quietly become a utilities company for knowledge work, albeit one with a much better slide deck.

That makes it powerful but not invincible. A significant share of Microsoft's software surface area can be rebuilt, self-hosted, or swapped for open alternatives, even if doing so remains inconvenient enough to preserve the moat for now.

moatNarrative: The moat is strongest where Microsoft controls organizational workflow, identity, procurement pathways, and default compatibility expectations.
GitHub, Azure, and Microsoft 365 reinforce each other into a platform stack that feels safer to keep paying than to rethink.
decentralizationNarrative: Productivity, version control, storage, and even cloud layers all have credible open or self-hosted alternatives.
The decentralization challenge is cultural and operational more than technical: Microsoft monetizes institutional inertia with exceptional discipline.
company.snapshotNote: Curated early-2026 public-source snapshot. Exact ordering can drift with the tape.

moat.rationale: Enterprise defaults, compliance comfort, and deeply embedded workflow software make Microsoft durable.

decentralizability.rationale: Open productivity, git hosting, and private cloud tooling are credible enough to create real replacement pressure.

profitability.rationale: Microsoft monetizes distribution, enterprise trust, and cloud scale with unusual consistency.

peRatio.rationale: Approximate valuation snapshot reflecting durable growth and margin quality.

marketCap.rationale: Approximate market cap snapshot from public market trackers.

products.name: Microsoft 365
products.summary: Subscription bundle for documents, collaboration, email, and enterprise workflows.
products.whyItMatters: This is one of Microsoft's most obvious rent-capture surfaces.
replacementSketch: Open office suites plus self-hosted collaboration are already good enough for more organizations than current procurement habits admit.
AI-mediated file conversion and workflow migration should keep lowering switching pain.

alternatives.name: LibreOffice
alternatives.summary: Open desktop productivity suite for documents, spreadsheets, and presentations.

openness.rationale: Excellent user control and open formats orientation.

decentralizationFit.rationale: Local-first productivity reduces dependence on subscription landlords.

readiness.rationale: Highly usable today, especially where exact Microsoft workflow fidelity is not mandatory.

costLeverage.rationale: One of the clearest examples of a mature free alternative compressing incumbents.

alternatives.name: Nextcloud + Collabora/ONLYOFFICE
alternatives.summary: Self-hosted collaboration stack for storage, docs, and workflows.

openness.rationale: Far more controllable than a closed SaaS productivity suite.

decentralizationFit.rationale: Can be run by the organization itself rather than a distant platform.

readiness.rationale: Quite usable, though adoption depends on operational tolerance and workflow needs.

costLeverage.rationale: Creates real negotiating leverage against per-seat software rent.

disruptionConcepts.name: Portable Workspace Federation
disruptionConcepts.summary: A work suite where documents, identity, collaboration, and billing are portable across self-hosted and managed providers instead of tied to Microsoft 365 rent capture.
disruptionConcepts.thesis: Unbundle office productivity into portable data, interoperable hosting, and competitive service layers.
disruptionConcepts.bitcoinOrDecentralizationRole: Lightning can settle plugin fees, storage overages, and collaboration services in a more open workspace economy.
disruptionConcepts.coordinationMechanism: Teams choose hosts, document engines, and automation providers independently while staying on shared document and identity standards.
disruptionConcepts.verificationOrTrustModel: Signed document histories, portable permission logs, and migration proofs reduce lock-in and fake interoperability claims.
adoptionPath: Start with teams already using mixed open tools
Push into larger organizations once workflow parity and migration tooling improve

decentralizationFit.rationale: This concept meaningfully shifts control away from a single incumbent operator.

coordinationCredibility.rationale: The participant and incentive model is plausible but still operationally demanding.

implementationFeasibility.rationale: Current tools and market structure could support an initial version without waiting for a full paradigm shift.

incumbentPressure.rationale: If adopted, the concept would chip away at pricing power or default distribution leverage.

disruptionConcepts.name: Office Appliance Cooperative
disruptionConcepts.summary: Local office appliances bundle docs, sync, communications, and lightweight compute into repairable hardware that small teams can own outright instead of renting an endless suite subscription.
disruptionConcepts.thesis: Unlike the first concept's portable federation, this one packages the alternative as a tangible local office system.
disruptionConcepts.bitcoinOrDecentralizationRole: Open hardware and reusable software stacks turn office productivity into something closer to owned infrastructure than rented tenancy.
disruptionConcepts.coordinationMechanism: Appliance vendors, local integrators, and support cooperatives compete while users keep files on open formats and sync layers.
disruptionConcepts.verificationOrTrustModel: Health checks, backup tests, and document round-trip validation show whether the appliance can really replace subscription dependence.
failureModes: Teams may still want Microsoft's compatibility guarantees
Local appliance support can become fragmented
adoptionPath: Start with smaller teams and sovereignty-sensitive organizations
Expand after file compatibility and admin tooling are boringly good

decentralizationFit.rationale: This concept decentralizes office productivity ownership into local appliances and open file workflows.

coordinationCredibility.rationale: The coordination loop is credible because integrators and appliance vendors can compete as long as documents remain portable.

implementationFeasibility.rationale: Most primitives already exist; the open suite pieces already exist, though enterprise compatibility still matters.

incumbentPressure.rationale: If it scales, it pressures Microsoft 365's recurring subscription hold and suite bundling.

products.name: GitHub
products.summary: Code hosting, collaboration, and developer workflow platform.
products.whyItMatters: GitHub's value comes from network effects in open-source and enterprise development workflows.
replacementSketch: Open Git hosting and decentralized forge concepts reduce dependence on a single social layer for software development.
AI-assisted migration can make self-hosting or federation much less operationally annoying.

alternatives.name: Gitea
alternatives.summary: Lightweight open forge for code hosting and collaboration.

decentralizationFit.rationale: Fits federated or self-hosted development workflows naturally.

readiness.rationale: Mature enough for many internal and community use cases.

costLeverage.rationale: A direct reminder that code hosting does not have to be socially monopolized.

alternatives.name: Self-hosted Git platforms
alternatives.summary: Umbrella category for self-hosted Git services such as Gitea and GitLab CE.

openness.rationale: Organizations can own the forge instead of borrowing it.

decentralizationFit.rationale: Moves developer coordination away from a single public platform.

readiness.rationale: Operationally straightforward for many engineering teams.

costLeverage.rationale: Useful where compliance or cost concerns justify the move.

disruptionConcepts.name: Federated Forge with Maintenance Bounties
disruptionConcepts.summary: A forge network where code hosting, identity, CI, and maintainer payments are portable across many operators instead of one dominant developer social graph.
disruptionConcepts.thesis: Break the combined lock-in of code hosting, social proof, and maintainer funding by making each layer portable.
disruptionConcepts.bitcoinOrDecentralizationRole: Lightning enables maintainer bounties, CI settlement, and sponsor flows without forcing all activity through one corporate billing surface.
disruptionConcepts.coordinationMechanism: Forges interoperate on issues, pull requests, and package identity while maintainers sell review and maintenance work in open markets.
disruptionConcepts.verificationOrTrustModel: Signed git histories, mirrored issue events, and escrowed bounty milestones reduce fake activity and unpaid work.
adoptionPath: Win first among open-source projects already uncomfortable with one-host dependency
Expand with better federation and sponsorship tooling

decentralizationFit.rationale: This concept meaningfully shifts control away from a single incumbent operator.

coordinationCredibility.rationale: The participant and incentive model is plausible but still operationally demanding.

implementationFeasibility.rationale: Current tools and market structure could support an initial version without waiting for a full paradigm shift.

incumbentPressure.rationale: If adopted, the concept would chip away at pricing power or default distribution leverage.

disruptionConcepts.name: Attested Build Artifact Market
disruptionConcepts.summary: Independent builders, testers, and artifact mirrors earn by producing reproducible build results and signed release bundles so trust in software distribution spreads beyond one forge.
disruptionConcepts.thesis: Unlike the first concept's maintenance bounty market, this one attacks GitHub's release and CI trust concentration.
disruptionConcepts.bitcoinOrDecentralizationRole: Open settlement rewards build, test, and mirror work directly instead of bundling it inside a single platform.
disruptionConcepts.coordinationMechanism: Projects publish build specs, providers offer execution and mirroring, and maintainers choose from competing verification results.
disruptionConcepts.verificationOrTrustModel: Reproducible builds, signed attestations, and quorum-based test results make it harder to fake a successful pipeline.
failureModes: Projects may not want to manage more CI vendors
Attestation standards have to stay simple enough for ordinary maintainers
adoptionPath: Start with high-value releases that already care about supply-chain trust
Expand as artifact and test markets become commodity services

decentralizationFit.rationale: This concept decentralizes build, test, and release trust across many providers instead of one forge platform.

coordinationCredibility.rationale: The coordination loop is credible because build jobs and attestations can be priced as discrete services with comparable outputs.

implementationFeasibility.rationale: Most primitives already exist; the tooling exists today, though standardizing attestations is still the work.

incumbentPressure.rationale: If it scales, it pressures GitHub's CI, release, and repository-trust concentration.
